title: Glassy-Jelly Egyptian Umiboshi Mushimono
description: A weeknight Egyptian dish featuring umeboshi and a low-glycemic-friendly glassy-jelly mustard sauce, steamed to perfection using the mushimono method.
instructions: In a small saucepan, combine rice vinegar, maple syrup, ginger, mustard seeds, garlic, salt, and water. Bring to a boil over medium heat, then reduce to low heat and simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the sauce through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, pressing on the solids to extract as much liquid as possible.
Add the turmeric, sugar-free jelly, coriander, and cilantro to the bowl and stir until the jelly has dissolved.
In a separate saucepan, bring a pot of water to a boil and blanch the umeboshi plums for 5 minutes. Drain and set aside.
To assemble the dish, place a few umeboshi plums on a plate and spoon the glassy-jelly sauce over them.
Steam the assembly using the mushimono method, covered with a clean towel, for 5 minutes, or until the sauce is set and the plums are tender.
